pattern: Each nonzero tower contributes its top, bottom, and four sides, and every shared face between a tower and a taller-or-equal neighbour hides two unit squares, so the total is the per-tower surface minus twice the overlaps.
hints: Start by counting every cube's exposed faces as if the towers stood alone, then subtract what gluing hides.
A tower of height v exposes 4v on its sides plus 2 for top and bottom, but only when v is positive.
Between two side-by-side towers, the buried area is twice the smaller height; sum those over each adjacent pair to the right and below to avoid double counting.

edge_inputs.description: hollow centre with a zero cell
tall border around a short centre
single zero cell has no surface
row with a gap between two towers

stress.description: random small grids with many zeros and varied heights
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(50, max(1, size)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 6) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: low ceiling keeps frequent zeros and shared faces

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):

    return [grid]
